summary: Internal link not removed in bullet list and numbered list on export

description.text:
### Description

The internal link is not removed in a bullet list and in a numbered list on export for both HTML and simple PDF. But is removed in the body of the text.

I get:

Author

* [[20250227141213]] Book title
 
Book title

1. [[20250227141213]] Book title

Book title

But is expecting:

Author

* Book title
 
Book title

1. Book title
 
Book title


### Reproducing

There are two markdown files attached. One for author and another for book. If I export the author one, for both HTML and simple-PDF. Same inconsistency.

My settings are:

Zettelkasten -> Internal links. Always.
Import and Export -> Export settings. (Checked) Use Zettlr's internal Pandoc for exports.
Import and Export -> Export settings. Internal links. (Selected) Remove internal links completely.

If I uncheck internal Pandoc option, same problem.

### Zettlr version

3.4.2

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] Intel 64bit
- [x] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

macOS Sequoia 15.3.1

### Additional Information

[20250227140727.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19002972/20250227140727.md)
[20250227141213.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19002973/20250227141213.md)

I have installed miktex. But when Export xelatex , something wrong." Pandoc exited with code 47

xelatex not found. Please select a different --pdf-engine or install xelatex"

summary: Add Button to auto-add all files to a project's export

description.text:
### Description

I can't export projects without selecting manually all the files to export, which is a problem if I have over 50 files.

I haven't used the projects feature in months, but in the past, all files were exported automatically, [which is also how it's described in the documentation](https://docs.zettlr.com/en/advanced/projects/#building-projects).

Now, going to Project Settings -> Files shows a message "You have not selected any file to export". I can't click "Export" on the Project contextual menu.

I tried reopening a project that worked in the past, with the same result.

### Reproducing

1. Open a project
2. Go to Project Settings, select Export Formats under "General"
3. Try to export the project: export can't be clicked
4. Go to Project Settings, under "Files", no files are selected

### Zettlr version

3.4.2

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 10

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Copy-pasting images doesn't prompt to save them as files anymore

description.text:
### Description

When copy pasting an image, I cannot save them as a file anymore, but I only obtain a relative path link. This is exactly what I want when I insert images from my computer. However, when I copy them from the internet, I get a link to "user\AppData\Local\Temp\image-file", which is not ideal since it only stores files temporarily.

"Copy image link" and pasting the URL works inside ![](), although not automatically with CTRL+shift+I.
Drag and drop from the file browser works too (thanks so much for solving that!).

I haven't found any way to change this behaviour in preferences (the path where they're automatically saved should be in the editor tab according to the docs).

### Reproducing

Copy image from local storage or website.
Paste into Zettlr.

### Zettlr version

3.4.2

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 11

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Citation auto-complete does not work in list items.

description.text:
### Description

When writing a list item, I can type `@` and filter my list of citations, per usual. However, when I hit `Enter`, instead of auto-completing the highlighted citation from the list, Zettlr instead creates the next list item.

For example, say I'm writing a list item and want to enter the citation `@westBuyNowHow2022`. In the example below, I begin typing the citation and, at the point that I've written `@westBuy`, the correct citation is highlighted in the auto-complete list…

```
- I am typing a list item and begin to enter this citation: @westBuy
```

…so I hit `Enter` and this is the result:

```
- I am typing a list item and begin to enter this citation: @westBuy
- 
```

…versus the expected result, which would be: 

```
- I am typing a list item and begin to enter this citation: @westBuyNowHow2022
```

…or, rendered:

```
- I am typing a list item and begin to enter this citation: West (2022)
```

### Reproducing

See above.

### Zettlr version

3.4.2-nightly from 2025-02-24

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Manjaro (Rolling)

### Additional Information

I use the appimage.

summary: Add to dictionary does not work

description.text:
### Description

I have tried several times to add a word ("Infrastructuring") to dictionary but it does not seem to work. As I add it, it appears in the list of words if I go to the options and spell checking. However, when I restart Zettlr, the word has disappeared from the list.

### Reproducing

- Add a word that is not recognized (e.g., "Infrastructuring") to a note. it should be underlined with a red line 
- Right-click and choose add to dictionary.
- The word is not underlined anymore, and if you go to preferences/spellchecking you can find the word at the end of the list in user dictionary.
- Restart Zettlr.
- The word is not there anymore and you have the red underlining back where it was.

### Zettlr version

3.4.1

### Installation Method

Other (please specify below)

### Installation Method (Other)

Auto update in the application.

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 11.

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Sorting options for TOC to manipulate document

description.text:
### Checklist

- [x] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it.
- [x] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this.

### Feature Proposal

I would like to change order of element blocks in the Table of Contents with the mouse by dragging and dropping, but is should be possible to do sorting for the whole document or single sections. The document should reflect this change and be rearranged accrdingly, just as it happens with drag and drop.

### Example Workflow

For example on a TOC like
```
# 1
## c
## a
## b

# 3
## b
## c
## a

# 2
## a
## c
## b
```
Sorting or reverse sorting by name should accomplish for exqample
```
# 1
## c
## a
## b

# 2
## a
## c
## b

# 3
## b
## c
## a
```
or
```
# 3
## c
## b
## a

# 2
## c
## b
## a

# 1
## c
## b
## a
```
or
```
# 1
## a
## b
## c

# 2
## a
## b
## c

# 3
## a
## b
## c
```
or
```
# 3
## a
## b
## c

# 2
## a
## b
## c

# 1
## a
## b
## c
```

### Caveats

None

### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself?

No

### Platforms

- [x] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: "Other files" from selected directory in file manager missing

description.text:
### Description

Hi,

I'm currently looking for an alternative to Obsidian, which I use for several xears now, but would like to move my vault to the cloud, which doesn't work well with Obsidians autosave feature. I tested Zettlr and like the features and the overall structure of the tool a lot, but I have some problems regarding the use of non-markdown files or "other files" as it is called in the tool.

I don't see any files that are in my project folder in the right panel, no matter the format (pdf, jpg, etc.). I tried it on Linux (Mint 22.1) and Windows 10 on 2 different computers. The funny thing is:, until the latest 2.x version of Zettlr (2.3.0), all files are visible and can be dragged into the markdown files for example, but since version 3.x all files vanished. I tried the AppImage and Flatpak versions on Linux and the Installer on Windows 10, with no difference.

I took a look in the log files, but there are no errors or anything obviously useful, no matter which verion of Zettlr. I also tinkered with all possibly related options in the preferences without success.

With kind regards
Marcus

### Reproducing

All versions since 3.x don't show any files other than markdown (.md) files. All versions up to 2.3.0 do.

### Zettlr version

2.3.0 - 3.4.1

### Installation Method

Other (please specify below)

### Installation Method (Other)

AppImage and Flathub on Linux, Installer on Windows 10

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Linux Mint 22.1, Windows 10

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Discord Rich Presence for accountability and peer pressure

description.text:
### Checklist

- [x] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it.
- [x] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this.

### Feature Proposal

Discord Rich Presence support would be absolutely a 'nice to have' and in no way a 'must have' but it would help easily distracted people like me, who are using peer support and peer pressure for getting things done with being accountable. 

Showing, as a Discord status, that Zettlr is open and ideally which document and also ideally if the user is active in said document (and not only have it idling in the background) would be helpful. 



### Example Workflow

* Join a Discord server/channel that is used for peer pressure/accountability
* State what you are working on and what is your goal
* open your Zettlr document
* your Discord status will now show the document you are working on and also show your activity status: active/idle
* actually doing your work instead of doomscrolling because there are people calling you out for not doing it

### Caveats

This might be useful only for a small number of Zettlr users and should be set to "off" by default because of privacy.

### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself?

No

### Platforms

- [x] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Additional Information

https://discord.com/developers/docs/rich-presence/overview

There are quite some examples how Discord Rich Presence could be implemented. Like for example:

* https://github.com/Radiicall/jellyfin-rpc
* https://www.mobileread.com/forums/showthread.php?t=357616
* https://github.com/lukeleppan/obsidian-discordrpc
